title: Cannot set sysctl net.unix.max_dgram_qlen
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. run a conteainer with parameters `--sysctl net.unix.max_dgram_qlen=1024`


**Describe the results you received:**

```
Error: open /proc/sys/net/unix/max_dgram_qlen: No such file or directory: OCI not found
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

Container starting with correct parameters

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

This works with docker but not with podman

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.9
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: Also, I tried to put the correct parameters on the host, but they don't get honored in the container

`    rootless: true`

That's probably the cause. I imagine you were running Docker as root, and are not running Podman rootless? I'm not the most familiar with what sysctls are allowed and disallowed without root, but it seems logical to assume that this is a root-only sysctl. Have you tried running Podman as root?

Nope, will do, it would make sense...
Although what surprised me is that the hosts parameters were not honored in the container
If I run my container with `--network=host` it works though, but I would prefer not using that option

that setting is exposed from the kernel only when the network namespace is owned by the initial user namespace (i.e. not running in a user namespace).

I'd expect it to fail with root Podman or Docker also when they are creating a user namespace.
I understand that, but again, what surprises me is that If I set it correctly system-wide in the kernel (outside of podman), then this setting is not honored inside podman containers =/
That is because you are in a different user namespace.  But this could be a kernel bug.  If this is not applied to the new network namespace.
